Think of this as a BFS problem: which indices can you reach with 1 jump, 2 jumps, etc.?
Track two boundaries: the end of the current 'level' (curEnd) and the farthest you can reach from this level.
When you reach curEnd, you must take another jump. Update curEnd to the farthest reachable position.
You only need O(1) space: track jumps, curEnd, and farthest. Iterate through the array once (excluding the last element).
